steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says music may have an infinite number of possible meanings, and "as long as the evidence a listener uses to support an interpretation is ____, it is difficult to justify the claim that the interpretation is incorrect." The blank must describe a quality of evidence that makes an interpretation defensible — something that validates rather than undermines the listener's reading.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'inferior': means of lower quality. Inferior evidence would make an interpretation easy to dismiss, the opposite of what the sentence says. ✗
  • Choice B — 'identical': means exactly the same. This describes whether evidence matches something else, not whether it is sound or valid. ✗
  • Choice C — 'repetitive': means saying the same thing repeatedly. This describes form, not the logical quality needed to defend an interpretation. ✗
  • Choice D — 'reasonable': means logically sound and fair. Evidence that is reasonable gives the listener a defensible basis for an interpretation, which is exactly why it would be difficult to call the interpretation incorrect. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
